title: Open Targets ETL (OT-own CC0 data; per-integrated-source license segregation)
context: Open Targets provides two categories of data: its own CC0-computed evidence/associations and aggregated third-party source data with varied licenses. Only OT-own CC0 data may be ingested into the CC0 core graph. Any integrated third-party source-level data must first pass the license gate independently. CC-BY or CC-BY-SA derived fields must land in segregated overlays. This task has higher complexity and risk due to the license segregation requirement.
objective: Implement an Open Targets ETL pipeline that ingests only OT-own CC0-computed evidence/associations, segregates any CC-BY/CC-BY-SA-derived fields into separate overlays with their own license manifests, normalizes identifiers, attaches complete provenance, and passes all CI gates without contaminating the CC0 core.
acceptanceCriteria: Ingests only Open Targets' own CC0-computed evidence/associations; no integrated third-party source-level record is ingested without that upstream source passing the license gate.
CC-BY and CC-BY-SA-derived fields land in segregated overlay files with their own license manifest, never mixed into the CC0 core.
Every edge carries complete provenance: source=Open Targets, data type, license, version, method, pipeline commit.
Output passes Biolink/SHACL validation and all CI gates.
Open Targets' allowlist entry (OT-own CC0 scope) is marked approved before ingestion begins.
ETL code documents the OT-own vs integrated-source boundary clearly in comments and README.
output: etl/opentargets/ directory containing the Open Targets ETL pipeline code with overlay segregation, committed via PR with CI green.
